transcript:
CALLER: Good afternoon. I'm calling to check whether United Health Care TPA is accepted at your clinic for a planned procedure.
AGENT: Good afternoon! Yes, United Health Care TPA is on our approved list. What procedure are you looking at?
CALLER: It's a laparoscopic cholecystectomy — gallbladder removal. My name is Geeta Venkataraman.
AGENT: Ms. Venkataraman, that procedure is covered under most TPA plans. You'll need a pre-authorization letter from your insurer before admission. Our TPA coordinator will help initiate that on your behalf if you come in for a pre-admission consultation.
CALLER: How far in advance do I need to do that?
AGENT: We recommend at least 3 to 5 working days before your surgery date. It gives enough time for back-and-forth with the insurance company if any documents are needed.
CALLER: Understood. And what documents do I bring for the consultation?
AGENT: Bring your insurance card, employee ID if it's a group policy, the surgeon's referral, and any relevant diagnostic reports like ultrasound results. Our TPA desk is open weekdays 9 AM to 5 PM.
CALLER: Thank you, very helpful.
AGENT: Happy to help, Ms. Venkataraman. Wishing you a smooth procedure!
